sessions.intent: Design Data envelope architecture — Engine.Types, Type context, Data restructure, envelope pipeline

before.assumptions: Type knowledge scattered across three static/instance sources. Data has no context. Type is eagerly derived. No transport serialization view exists.
before.risk: Large architectural change touching Data (universal type), Type, and TypeMapping. Phased approach mitigates — each phase independently shippable.

sessions.plan: Four-phase design: (1) Engine.Types replaces static TypeMapping, (2) Type gets context + lazy derivation, Data gets late-bound context, (3) Data partial class split + Out view, (4) Envelope pipeline with Wrap/Compress/Encrypt chain. OBP throughout.

after.status: Design complete. Four-phase plan ready for coder handoff.
after.health: Good. No code changes, pure design.
after.notes: Coder should read plan.md + learnings.md before starting Phase 1.

sessions.intent: Implement Phase 1: Engine.Types — consolidate scattered type knowledge into a single live instance on Engine

before.assumptions: Type knowledge is scattered across static TypeMapping (Engine/Utility), FileModule.TypeMapping (instance), and MimeTypeHelper (Utils). Data.cs, file/types.cs, and StepActions reference static TypeMapping directly.
before.risk: Phase 1 is additive — no existing behavior changes. Risk is low.

sessions.plan: Create PLang/Runtime2/Engine/Types/this.cs with all type knowledge. Add Types property to Engine. Add EngineTypes global alias. Write 62 TUnit tests. Keep old static TypeMapping as-is until Phase 2.

actions.context: New @this class consolidating PLang name<->CLR type, extension->Kind, extension->MIME, Kind->compressibility. OBP-compliant API with noun names.
Added Types property to Engine, EngineTypes global alias to both PLang and PLang.Tests.
62 TUnit tests covering Clr, Name, Kind, Mime, Compressible, Add/Remove, .key conflict, generics, nullable, MIME resolution, Engine integration.
Plan, version summary, and bot root summary.

after.status: Phase 1 complete. Engine.Types created with full type knowledge. 62 tests pass, 78 existing TypeMapping tests still pass.
after.health: Green. Build succeeds with 0 errors. All tests pass. No regressions.
after.notes: Phase 2 next: Type gets context + lazy derivation, Data gets late-bound context.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v1 — verify 62 Engine.Types tests catch real bugs, not just confirm implementation

before.assumptions: Coder wrote 62 tests for a new class. Tests likely verify the happy path. Need to check for false greens, missing edge cases, and untested public methods.
before.risk: If tests are false greens, Phase 2 builds on an untested foundation. Bugs compound silently.

sessions.plan: Run C# test suite, analyze each test group for intent verification vs implementation mirroring, identify coverage gaps (especially BuilderNames/ComplexSchemas), check for edge case coverage, write test-report.json.

actions.context: Analyzed 62 tests for intent verification, false greens, and coverage gaps. Experimentally verified 2 bugs: null crash in Kind/Mime and backtick in Name() for generic types.
8 findings: 1 critical, 3 major, 4 minor. Verdict: needs-fixes.

after.status: Test quality analysis complete. 8 findings in test-report.json. Verdict: needs-fixes.
after.health: Tests are mostly solid but have 1 crash path (Kind/Mime null), 1 false green (Name backtick), and 2 untested public methods (BuilderNames, ComplexSchemas).
after.notes: Fix findings #1-#4 before passing to auditor. The null crash is most urgent.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v2 — Phase 2 context + lazy derivation tests, plus tracking v1 carry-forward issues

before.assumptions: Coder added Phase 2 (context, lazy derivation) with 23 new tests. v1 findings likely not addressed since coder focused on new functionality.
before.risk: Add() method may not update derived data structures (KindOf depends on _allKinds/_mimeToKind built at construction time). Phase 2 context wiring is complex — subtle bugs in stamping chain could be masked by static TypeMapping fallback.

sessions.plan: Run tests, verify v1 findings carry forward, analyze Phase 2 test quality, check Add→KindOf pipeline, check Methods.cs coverage, write test-report.json.

actions.context: Full Phase 2 test quality analysis. Found critical bug: Add() doesn't update _allKinds/_mimeToKind, breaking KindOf for dynamic extensions. Verified experimentally. v1 findings all carry forward.
Updated to v2: 8 findings (2 critical, 3 major, 3 minor). Verdict: needs-fixes.

after.status: v2 test analysis complete. Found real bug in Add→KindOf pipeline. v1 findings carry forward with escalated impact.
after.health: 1324 tests pass but Add() is broken for KindOf(). Phase 2 context chain is well-tested but has a masking problem (fallback to TypeMapping makes it hard to verify context path is used).
after.notes: Critical: Add() must update _allKinds and _mimeToKind. Must fix before Phase 3.

sessions.intent: Verify v2 fixes + test quality analysis of Phase 3 (Data partial class split + Out view)

before.assumptions: Coder fixed v2 critical/major findings and implemented Phase 3. Fixes should address Add→KindOf, null guards, backtick, BuilderNames/ComplexSchemas. Phase 3 is structural — no behavior change expected.
before.risk: Low. Phase 3 is a partial class split. Main risk is that field access across partials breaks compilation, but tests would catch that.

sessions.plan: Run tests, verify fixes with new tests, analyze Phase 3 test quality, write test-report.json.

actions.context: Verified 16 new fix tests: Add→KindOf (4), null guards (4), backtick (2), BuilderNames/ComplexSchemas (6). All fixes correct.
Phase 3 partial class split: clean structural refactor. 8 new envelope/attribute tests. All existing tests pass unchanged.
Updated to v3: 3 minor findings only. Verdict: approved.

after.status: All v2 critical/major findings verified fixed. Phase 3 clean. Verdict: approved for auditor.
after.health: 1349 tests pass, 0 failures. Code and tests are solid. Only minor findings remain.
after.notes: Ready for auditor review. Phase 4 (envelope pipeline) will need serializer-level tests for [Out] attribute.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis of coder v4 — Phase 4 envelope pipeline (Wrap/Compress/Encrypt/Decrypt/Decompress/Unwrap)

before.assumptions: Coder added Phase 4 with 17 new tests. Pipeline involves serialization, compression, and rehydration — multiple failure modes possible at the transport boundary.
before.risk: Decompress is the highest-risk method — it handles untrusted data from the wire. If error handling is incomplete, corrupt data crashes the runtime.

sessions.plan: Run tests, analyze 17 pipeline tests for quality, check error paths in Decompress, verify RehydrateNestedData correctness, check round-trip data fidelity, write test-report.json.

actions.context: Analyzed 17 Phase 4 tests. Found critical: Decompress() has no exception handling around GZipDecompress/JsonSerializer.Deserialize. Found major: all Decompress error paths untested, standalone Decompress test has weak assertions. Round-trip tests are solid.
Updated to v4: 7 findings (1 critical, 2 major, 4 minor). Verdict: needs-fixes.

after.status: Phase 4 test analysis complete. Critical finding: Decompress has no exception handling at transport boundary. Verdict: needs-fixes.
after.health: 1366 tests pass but Decompress will crash on corrupt data instead of returning Error. Error paths are completely untested.
after.notes: Fix finding #1 (exception handling in Decompress) and add error path tests (#2) before auditor reviews. Round-trip happy path is solid.

sessions.intent: Fix all 7 tester v4 findings — exception handling in Decompress, error path tests, weak assertions, edge case coverage

before.assumptions: Tester found 1 critical (Decompress unhandled exceptions), 2 major (untested error paths, weak assertions), 4 minor (multi-level nesting, Wrap context, Properties loss, Compress on unwrapped). All findings are valid.
before.risk: Low. Fixes are additive — try/catch in Decompress, new tests, doc comments. No behavioral change to happy path.

sessions.plan: Fix #1: wrap GZipDecompress+Deserialize in try/catch returning FromError. Fix #2: add 4 error path tests. Fix #3: strengthen Decompress assertion. Fix #4: multi-level nesting test. Fix #5: Wrap context assertion. Fix #6: Properties loss comment + test. Fix #7: document Compress behavior.

actions.context: Added try/catch around GZipDecompress+Deserialize catching InvalidDataException and JsonException separately. Added doc comment about Properties not surviving compression cycle.
7 new tests: Decompress error paths (4), multi-level nesting (1), Properties loss (1). Strengthened existing assertions: Decompress value verification, Wrap context propagation.

after.status: All 7 tester v4 findings fixed. 1372 tests pass, 0 failures.
after.health: Green. Decompress now handles corrupt data gracefully at the transport boundary. All error paths tested.
